questions.question: Laws of reflection of light is applicable in which among the following types of surfaces?
questions.explanation: (i) The angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection. (ii) The incident ray, the normal to the mirror at the point of incidence and the reflected ray, all lie in the same plane. These laws of reflection are applicable to all types of reflecting surfaces including plane and spherical surfaces- concave and convex.

questions.question: SI Unit of Power of lens is ______?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of power of a lens is 'dioptre'. It is denoted by the letter D. If f is expressed in metres, then, power is expressed in dioptres. Thus, 1 dioptre is the power of a lens whose focal length is 1 metre.

questions.question: Direction of electric current is taken as opposite to the direction of the flow of ______?
questions.explanation: Conventionally, in an electric circuit the direction of electric current is taken as opposite to the direction of the flow of electrons, which are negative charges.

questions.question: The SI unit of electric charge is ________?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of electric charge is coulomb (C), which is equivalent to the charge contained in nearly 6 X 1018 electrons.

questions.question: Which among the following property(ies) change with change in gravity?
questions.explanation: The mass of an object remains the same everywhere, that is, on the earth and on any planet whereas its weight depends on its location. Because weight = mass * gravity. Inertia also doesn't change as it is depend on mass.

questions.question: Which among the following force is governed by Coulombs law?
questions.explanation: Electromagnetic force is the force between charged particles. In the simpler case, when charges are at rest, the force is governed by Coulomb's law: attractive for unlike charges and repulsive for like charges. Charges in motion produce magnetic effects and a magnetic field gives rise to a force on a moving charge.

questions.question: The motion of the moon around the earth is governed by ________?
questions.explanation: The motion of the moon around the earth is due to the centripetal force. The centripetal force is provided by the force of attraction of the earth.

questions.question: Twinkling of star is an effect of which among the following phenomenon?
questions.explanation: The twinkling of a star is due to atmospheric refraction of starlight. The starlight, on entering the earth's atmosphere, undergoes refraction continuously before it reaches the earth.

questions.question: Which among the following lens is used for correction myopic condition?
questions.explanation: Myopia is also known as near-sightedness. A person with myopia can see nearby objects clearly but cannot see distant objects distinctly. This defect can be corrected by using a concave lens of suitable power.

questions.question: Which type of action takes place to produce nuclear energy?
questions.explanation: In a process called nuclear fission, the nucleus of a heavy atom (such as uranium, plutonium or thorium), when bombarded with low-energy neutrons, can be split apart into lighter nuclei, thereby producing nuclear energy.

questions.question: Rain will be classified as acid rain if the pH of the rain water is less than ________?
questions.explanation: When pH of rain water is less than 5.6, it is called acid rain.

questions.question: Bleaching power has chlorine and which among the following chemical element?
questions.explanation: Bleaching powder is produced by the action of chlorine on dry slaked lime, which is a compound of calcium. Bleaching powder is represented as CaOCl2.

questions.question: Which among the following is the most reactive of all other mentioned metals?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p2399/image001.png)

questions.question: Bonds which are formed by the sharing of an electron pair between two atoms is referred to as ______?
questions.explanation: Bonds which are formed by the sharing of an electron pair between two atoms are known as covalent bonds. Covalently bonded molecules are seen to have strong bonds within the molecule, but intermolecular forces are small.

questions.question: The arrangement of elements in Modern Periodic table is based on _________?
questions.explanation: Modern periodic table is based on atomic number of the elements. In 1913, Henry Moseley showed that the atomic number of an element is a more fundamental property than its atomic mass as described below. Accordingly, Mendeleev's Periodic Law was modified and atomic number was adopted as the basis of Modern Periodic Table.

questions.question: What is the chemical name of Mohr's Salt?
questions.explanation: Mohr's salt or ammonium ferrous sulfate is the inorganic compound. It is used in the Fricke's dosemeter to measure high doses of gamma rays.

questions.question: Partially hydrated form of which among the following compound is referred to as "Plaster of Paris"?
questions.explanation: On heating gypsum at 373 K, it loses water molecules and becomes calcium sulphate hemihydrate. This is called Plaster of paris, the substance which doctors use as plaster for supporting fractured bones in the right position. Plaster of Paris is a white powder and on mixing with water, it changes to gypsum once again giving a hard-solid mass.

questions.question: Which among the following metal is used in galvanization of steel?
questions.explanation: Galvanisation is a method of protecting steel and iron from rusting by coating them with a thin layer of zinc.

questions.question: Atomic theory was propounded by __________?
questions.explanation: John Dalton developed an atomic theory in the 1803. His theory was based on the laws of chemical combination. Dalton's atomic theory provided an explanation for the law of conservation of mass and the law of definite proportions.

questions.question: Atomicity refers to ________?
options: Types of atoms in a molecule
Density of atoms in a molecule
Number of atoms in a molecule
questions.explanation: The number of atoms constituting a molecule is known as its atomicity.

questions.question: Which among the following is Unicellular organisms?
questions.explanation: Amoeba, Chlamydomonas, Paramoecium are unicellular organisms. The main groups of unicellular organisms are bacteria, archaea, protozoa, unicellular algae, and unicellular fungi.

questions.question: Plasmolysis in a plant cell refers to __________?
options: Development of cells wall around the cell
questions.explanation: When a living plant cell loses water through osmosis there is shrinkage or contraction of the contents of the cell away from the cell wall. This phenomenon is known as plasmolysis.

questions.question: Valves in veins serve which among the following purposes?
options: Absorb nutrients from blood flowing through veins
Easy the flow of blood into heart chamber
questions.explanation: Veins collect the blood from different organs and bring it back to the heart. They do not need thick walls because the blood is no longer under pressure, instead they have valves that ensure that the blood flows only in one direction.

questions.question: Which among the following carries digested fat from intestine?
questions.explanation: Lymph carries digested and absorbed fat from intestine and drains excess fluid from extra cellular space back into the blood. It is similar to the plasma of blood but colourless and contains less protein.

questions.question: Leprosy is caused by __________?
questions.explanation: Leprosy, also known as Hansen's disease (HD), is a long-term infection by the bacterium Mycobacterium leprae.

questions.question: Method of staining individual nerve and cell structures is referred to as ________?
questions.explanation: Method of staining individual nerve and cell structures is referred to as the 'black reaction'. It was invented by Camillo Golgi. Golgi apparatus was first described by Camillo Golgi.

questions.question: Reproduction through regenerative cells is referred to as _________?
questions.explanation: Regenerative cells are used for reproduction in the process of budding. In Hydra, a bud develops as an outgrowth due to repeated cell division at one specific site.

questions.question: Which part of a plant show positive geotropism?
questions.explanation: The upward and downward growth of shoots and roots, respectively, in response to the pull of earth or gravity is referred to as geotropism. While shoot respond negatively, roots respond positively.

questions.question: which among the following is synthesized with the help of DNA in the cell nucleus?
questions.explanation: The DNA in the cell nucleus is the information source for making proteins. If the information is changed, different proteins will be made. Different proteins will eventually lead to altered body designs.

questions.question: Muscle cramps are caused due to which among the following actions?
options: Aerobic respiration leading to production of alcohol
Aerobic respiration leading to production of acid
Anaerobic respiration leading to production of alcohol
Anaerobic respiration leading to production of acid
Breakdown of pyruvate in aerobic reaction
questions.explanation: When there is a lack of oxygen in our muscle cells, anaerobic pathway for the break-down of pyruvate is taken. Here the pyruvate is converted into lactic acid which is also a three-carbon molecule. This build-up of lactic acid in our muscles during sudden activity causes cramps.
